macros.description: A macro to specify data alignment.
remarks:
This informs the compiler that a given datatype or variable must be
aligned to a specific byte count.
For example:

On compilers without alignment support, this macro is defined to an
invalid symbol, to make it clear that the current compiler is likely to
generate incorrect code when it sees this macro.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.4.0.

macros.name: SDL_ALLOC_SIZE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro to tag a function as returning a certain allocation.
remarks:
This is a hint to the compiler that a function allocates and returns
a specific amount of memory based on one of its arguments. For example,
the C runtime's malloc() function could use this macro with an argument
of 1 (first argument to malloc is the size of the allocation).

On compilers without alloc_size support, this is defined to
nothing.
Most apps don't need to, and should not, use this directly.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

Defined if (and only if) the compiler supports PowerPC Altivec
intrinsics.
remarks:
If this macro is defined, SDL will have already included
 <altivec.h>
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ANALYZER_NORETURN
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
A macro to tag a function as never-returning (for analysis
purposes).
remarks:
This is almost identical to SDL_NORETURN , except functions marked with
this can actually return. The difference is that this isn't
used for code generation, but rather static analyzers use this
information to assume truths about program state and available code
paths. Specifically, this tag is useful for writing an assertion
mechanism. Indeed, SDL_assert uses this
tag behind the scenes. Generally, apps that don't understand the
specific use-case for this tag should avoid using it directly.

On compilers without analyzer_noreturn support, this is defined to
nothing.

This symbol is used in SDL's headers, but apps and other libraries
are welcome to use it for their own interfaces as well.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ANDROID_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_READ
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: See the official Android developer guide for more information: http://developer.android.com/guide/topics/data/data-storage.html
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ANDROID_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_WRITE

macros.description: See the official Android developer guide for more information: http://developer.android.com/guide/topics/data/data-storage.html
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_arraysize
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: The number of elements in a static array.
remarks:
This will compile but return incorrect results for a pointer to an
array; it has to be an array the compiler knows the size of.

This macro looks like it double-evaluates the argument, but it does
so inside of sizeof , so there are no side-effects here, as
expressions do not actually run any code in these cases.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_assert
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
An assertion test that is normally performed only in debug
builds.
remarks:
This macro is enabled when the SDL_ASSERT_LEVEL is >= 2, otherwise
it is disabled. This is meant to only do these tests in debug builds, so
they can tend to be more expensive, and they are meant to bring
everything to a halt when they fail, with the programmer there to assess
the problem.

In short: you can sprinkle these around liberally and assume they
will evaporate out of the build when building for end-users.

When assertions are disabled, this wraps condition in a
 sizeof operator, which means any function calls and side
effects will not run, but the compiler will not complain about any
otherwise-unused variables that are only referenced in the
assertion.

One can set the environment variable " SDL_ASSERT " to one of several strings
("abort", "break", "retry", "ignore", "always_ignore") to force a
default behavior, which may be desirable for automation purposes. If
your platform requires GUI interfaces to happen on the main thread but
you're debugging an assertion in a background thread, it might be
desirable to set this to "break" so that your debugger takes control as
soon as assert is triggered, instead of risking a bad UI interaction
(deadlock, etc) in the application.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_assert_always
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: An assertion test that is always performed.
remarks:
This macro is always enabled no matter what SDL_ASSERT_LEVEL is set to. You almost
never want to use this, as it could trigger on an end-user's system,
crashing your program.

One can set the environment variable " SDL_ASSERT " to one of several strings
("abort", "break", "retry", "ignore", "always_ignore") to force a
default behavior, which may be desirable for automation purposes. If
your platform requires GUI interfaces to happen on the main thread but
you're debugging an assertion in a background thread, it might be
desirable to set this to "break" so that your debugger takes control as
soon as assert is triggered, instead of risking a bad UI interaction
(deadlock, etc) in the application.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ASSERT_CAPABILITY
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Wrapper around Clang thread safety analysis annotations.
remarks: Please see https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#mutex-h
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ASSERT_FILE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
A macro that reports the current file being compiled, for use in
assertions.
remarks:
This macro is only defined if it isn't already defined, so to
override it (perhaps with something that doesn't provide path
information at all, so build machine information doesn't leak into
public binaries), apps can define this macro before including SDL_assert .h. For example, defining this to
 "" will make sure no source path information is included in
asserts.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.4.0.

macros.name: SDL_ASSERT_LEVEL
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: The level of assertion aggressiveness.
remarks:
This value changes depending on compiler options and other
preprocessor defines.

It is currently one of the following values, but future SDL releases
might add more:
0: All SDL assertion macros are disabled.

1: Release settings: SDL_assert
disabled, SDL_assert_release
enabled.
2: Debug settings: SDL_assert and SDL_assert_release enabled.
3: Paranoid settings: All SDL assertion macros enabled, including SDL_assert_paranoid .
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_assert_paranoid
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
An assertion test that is performed only when built with paranoid
settings.
remarks:
This macro is enabled when the SDL_ASSERT_LEVEL is >= 3, otherwise
it is disabled. This is a higher level than both release and debug, so
these tests are meant to be expensive and only run when specifically
looking for extremely unexpected failure cases in a special build.

When assertions are disabled, this wraps condition in a
 sizeof operator, which means any function calls and side
effects will not run, but the compiler will not complain about any
otherwise-unused variables that are only referenced in the
assertion.

One can set the environment variable " SDL_ASSERT " to one of several strings
("abort", "break", "retry", "ignore", "always_ignore") to force a
default behavior, which may be desirable for automation purposes. If
your platform requires GUI interfaces to happen on the main thread but
you're debugging an assertion in a background thread, it might be
desirable to set this to "break" so that your debugger takes control as
soon as assert is triggered, instead of risking a bad UI interaction
(deadlock, etc) in the application.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_assert_release
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: An assertion test that is performed even in release builds.
remarks:
This macro is enabled when the SDL_ASSERT_LEVEL is >= 1, otherwise
it is disabled. This is meant to be for tests that are cheap to make and
extremely unlikely to fail; generally it is frowned upon to have an
assertion failure in a release build, so these assertions generally need
to be of more than life-and-death importance if there's a chance they
might trigger. You should almost always consider handling these cases
more gracefully than an assert allows.

When assertions are disabled, this wraps condition in a
 sizeof operator, which means any function calls and side
effects will not run, but the compiler will not complain about any
otherwise-unused variables that are only referenced in the
assertion.

One can set the environment variable " SDL_ASSERT " to one of several strings
("abort", "break", "retry", "ignore", "always_ignore") to force a
default behavior, which may be desirable for automation purposes. If
your platform requires GUI interfaces to happen on the main thread but
you're debugging an assertion in a background thread, it might be
desirable to set this to "break" so that your debugger takes control as
soon as assert is triggered, instead of risking a bad UI interaction
(deadlock, etc) in the application. *
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ASSERT_SHARED_CAPABILITY
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Wrapper around Clang thread safety analysis annotations.
remarks: Please see https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#mutex-h
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_AssertBreakpoint
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: The macro used when an assertion triggers a breakpoint.
remarks: This isn't for direct use by apps; use SDL_assert or SDL_TriggerBreakpoint instead.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_AtomicDecRef
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Decrement an atomic variable used as a reference count.
macros.returns:
Returns true if the variable reached zero after decrementing, false
otherwise.
remarks:
Note: If you don't know what this macro is for, you
shouldn't use it!
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_AtomicIncRef
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Increment an atomic variable used as a reference count.
macros.returns: Returns the previous value of the atomic variable.
remarks:
Note: If you don't know what this macro is for, you
shouldn't use it!
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

16.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.

macros.description: Retrieve the size, in bytes, from an SDL_AudioFormat .
macros.returns: Returns data size in bytes.
remarks:
For example, SDL_AUDIO_BYTESIZE(SDL_AUDIO_S16) returns

macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_AUDIO_DEVICE_DEFAULT_PLAYBACK
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.

Several functions that require an SDL_AudioDeviceID will accept this

macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_AUDIO_DEVICE_DEFAULT_RECORDING

macros.name: SDL_AUDIO_FRAMESIZE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Calculate the size of each audio frame (in bytes) from an SDL_AudioSpec .
macros.returns: Returns the number of bytes used per sample frame.
remarks:
This reports on the size of an audio sample frame: stereo Sint16 data (2 channels of 2 bytes each) would be
4 bytes per frame, for example.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_AUDIO_ISBIGENDIAN

macros.returns: Returns non-zero if format is bigendian, zero otherwise.

For example, SDL_AUDIO_ISBIGENDIAN(SDL_AUDIO_S16LE)
returns 0.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.

macros.name: SDL_AUDIO_MASK_BITSIZE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
Mask of bits in an SDL_AudioFormat
that contains the format bit size.
remarks:

macros.name: SDL_BYTEORDER

macros.description: A macro that reports the target system's byte order.
remarks:
This is set to either SDL_LIL_ENDIAN or SDL_BIG_ENDIAN (and maybe other values in
the future, if something else becomes popular). This can be tested with
the preprocessor, so decisions can be made at compile time.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_BYTESPERPIXEL
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro to determine an SDL_PixelFormat 's bytes per pixel.
macros.returns: Returns the bytes-per-pixel of format .
remarks:
Note that this macro double-evaluates its parameter, so do not use
expressions with side-effects here.

FourCC formats do their best here, but many of them don't have a
meaningful measurement of bytes per pixel.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_CACHELINE_SIZE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A guess for the cacheline size used for padding.
remarks:
Most x86 processors have a 64 byte cache line. The 64-bit PowerPC
processors have a 128 byte cache line. We use the larger value to be
generally safe.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_CAPABILITY
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Wrapper around Clang thread safety analysis annotations.
remarks: Please see https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#mutex-h
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_clamp
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Return a value clamped to a range.
macros.returns: Returns x, clamped between a and b.
remarks:
If x is outside the range a values between
 a and b , the returned value will be
 a or b as appropriate. Otherwise,
 x is returned.

This macro will produce incorrect results if b is less
than a .

This is a helper macro that might be more clear than writing out the
comparisons directly, and works with any type that can be compared with
the < and > operators. However, it
double-evaluates all its parameters, so do not use expressions with
side-effects here.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.returns:
Returns the SDL_ColorType for
 cspace .
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.

macros.name: SDL_COMPILE_TIME_ASSERT
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A compile-time assertion.
remarks:
This can check constant values known to the compiler at build
time for correctness, and end the compile with the error if they
fail.

Often times these are used to verify basic truths, like the size of a
datatype is what is expected:

SDL_COMPILE_TIME_ASSERT ( uint32_size , sizeof ( Uint32 ) == 4 );
 The name parameter must be a valid C symbol, and must be
unique across all compile-time asserts in the same compilation unit (one
run of the compiler), or the build might fail with cryptic errors on
some targets. This is used with a C language trick that works on older
compilers that don't support better assertion techniques.

If you need an assertion that operates at runtime, on variable data,
you should try SDL_assert instead.
macros.threadSafety: This macro doesn't generate any code to run.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_CompilerBarrier
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Mark a compiler barrier.
remarks:
A compiler barrier prevents the compiler from reordering reads and
writes to globally visible variables across the call.

This macro only prevents the compiler from reordering reads and
writes, it does not prevent the CPU from reordering reads and writes.
However, all of the atomic operations that modify memory are full memory
barriers.
macros.threadSafety:
Obviously this macro is safe to use from any thread at any time, but
if you find yourself needing this, you are probably dealing with some
very sensitive code; be careful!
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_const_cast
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Handle a Const Cast properly whether using C or C++.
macros.syntax: #define SDL_const_cast(type, expression) const_cast<type>(expression) /* or `((type)(expression))` in C */
macros.returns: Returns expression , cast to type .
remarks:
If compiled as C++, this macro offers a proper C++
const_cast<>.
If compiled as C, this macro does a normal C-style cast.
This is helpful to avoid compiler warnings in C++.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_copyp
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro to copy memory between objects, with basic type checking.
macros.syntax:
#define SDL_copyp(dst, src) \
 { SDL_COMPILE_TIME_ASSERT(SDL_copyp, sizeof (*(dst)) == sizeof (*(src))); } \
 SDL_memcpy((dst), (src), sizeof(*(src)))
remarks:
SDL_memcpy and SDL_memmove do not care where you copy
memory to and from, which can lead to bugs. This macro aims to avoid
most of those bugs by making sure that the source and destination are
both pointers to objects that are the same size. It does not check that
the objects are the same type , just that the copy will not
overflow either object.

The size check happens at compile time, and the compiler will throw
an error if the objects are different sizes.
Generally this is intended to copy a single object, not an array.

This macro looks like it double-evaluates its parameters, but the
extras them are in sizeof sections, which generate no code
nor side-effects.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
macros.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_CPUPauseInstruction
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
A macro to insert a CPU-specific "pause" instruction into the
program.
remarks:
This can be useful in busy-wait loops, as it serves as a hint to the
CPU as to the program's intent; some CPUs can use this to do more
efficient processing. On some platforms, this doesn't do anything, so
using this macro might just be a harmless no-op.

Note that if you are busy-waiting, there are often more-efficient
approaches with other synchronization primitives: mutexes, semaphores,
condition variables, etc.
macros.threadSafety: This macro is safe to use from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_DEBUG_TEXT_FONT_CHARACTER_SIZE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: The size, in pixels, of a single SDL_RenderDebugText () character.
remarks:
The font is monospaced and square, so this applies to all
characters.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_DECLSPEC
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro to tag a symbol as a public API.
remarks:
SDL uses this macro for all its public functions. On some targets, it
is used to signal to the compiler that this function needs to be
exported from a shared library, but it might have other side
effects.

This symbol is used in SDL's headers, but apps and other libraries
are welcome to use it for their own interfaces as well.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_DEFINE_AUDIO_FORMAT
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
Define an SDL_AudioFormat
value.
macros.returns: Returns a format value in the style of SDL_AudioFormat .
remarks:
SDL does not support custom audio formats, so this macro is not of
much use externally, but it can be illustrative as to what the various
bits of an SDL_AudioFormat mean.

For example, SDL_AUDIO_S32LE looks
like this:
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_DEFINE_COLORSPACE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro for defining custom SDL_Colorspace formats.
macros.syntax:
#define SDL_DEFINE_COLORSPACE(type, range, primaries, transfer, matrix, chroma) \
 (((Uint32)(type) << 28) | ((Uint32)(range) << 24) | ((Uint32)(chroma) << 20) | \
 ((Uint32)(primaries) << 10) | ((Uint32)(transfer) << 5) | ((Uint32)(matrix) << 0))
macros.returns: Returns a format value in the style of SDL_Colorspace .
remarks:
For example, defining SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB looks like
this:
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_DEFINE_PIXELFORMAT
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.

macros.returns: Returns a format value in the style of SDL_PixelFormat .
remarks:
For example, defining SDL_PIXELFORMAT_RGBA8888 looks

macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.

macros.name: SDL_DEPRECATED
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro to tag a symbol as deprecated.
remarks:
A function is marked deprecated by adding this macro to its
declaration:

extern SDL_DEPRECATED int ThisFunctionWasABadIdea ( void );
 Compilers with deprecation support can give a warning when a
deprecated function is used. This symbol may be used in SDL's headers,
but apps are welcome to use it for their own interfaces as well.

SDL, on occasion, might deprecate a function for various reasons.
However, SDL never removes symbols before major versions, so deprecated
interfaces in SDL3 will remain available until SDL4, where it would be
expected an app would have to take steps to migrate anyhow.

On compilers without a deprecation mechanism, this is defined to
nothing, and using a deprecated function will not generate a
warning.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_disabled_assert
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: The macro used when an assertion is disabled.
remarks:
This isn't for direct use by apps, but this is the code that is
inserted when an SDL_assert is disabled
(perhaps in a release build).

The code does nothing, but wraps condition in a sizeof
operator, which generates no code and has no side effects, but avoid
compiler warnings about unused variables.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
Add a note that your application has dynamic shared library
dependencies.
macros.syntax:
#define SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN(feature, description, priority, ...) \
 SDL_ELF_NOTE_INTERNAL( \
 "[{\"feature\":\"" feature \
 "\",\"description\":\"" description \
 "\",\"priority\":\"" priority \
 "\",\"soname\":" SDL_DLNOTE_JSON_ARRAY(__VA_ARGS__) "}]", \
 SDL_DLNOTE_UNIQUE_NAME);
remarks: You can do this by adding the following to the global scope:

SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN (
 "png" ,
 "Support for loading PNG images using libpng (required for APNG)" ,
 SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN_PRIORITY_RECOMMENDED ,
 "libpng12.so.0"
 )
 A trailing semicolon is not needed.

Or if you support multiple versions of a library, you can list
them:

// Our app supports SDL1, SDL2, and SDL3 by dynamically loading them
 SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN (
 "SDL" ,
 "Create windows through SDL video backend" ,
 SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN_PRIORITY_REQUIRED
 "libSDL-1.2.so.0" , "libSDL2-2.0.so.0" , "libSDL3.so.0"
 )
 This macro is not available for compilers that do not support
variadic macro's.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.4.0.

macros.name: SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN_PRIORITY_RECOMMENDED
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
Use this macro with SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN () to note that a
dynamic shared library dependency is recommended.
remarks:
Important functionality needs the dependency, the binary will work
but in most cases the dependency should be provided.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.4.0.

macros.name: SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN_PRIORITY_REQUIRED
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
Use this macro with SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN () to note that a
dynamic shared library dependency is required.
remarks:
Core functionality needs the dependency, the binary will not work if
it cannot be found.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.4.0.

macros.name: SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN_PRIORITY_SUGGESTED
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
Use this macro with SDL_ELF_NOTE_DLOPEN () to note that a
dynamic shared library dependency is optional.
remarks:
Optional functionality uses the dependency, the binary will work and
the dependency is only needed for full-featured installations.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.4.0.

macros.name: SDL_enabled_assert
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: The macro used when an assertion is enabled.
remarks:
This isn't for direct use by apps, but this is the code that is
inserted when an SDL_assert is
enabled.
The do {} while(0) avoids dangling else problems:

if ( x ) SDL_assert ( y ); else blah ();
 ... without the do/while, the "else" could attach to this macro's
"if". We try to handle just the minimum we need here in a macro...the
loop, the static vars, and break points. The heavy lifting is handled in
 SDL_ReportAssertion ().
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_EXCLUDES
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Wrapper around Clang thread safety analysis annotations.
remarks: Please see https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#mutex-h
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FALLTHROUGH
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
A macro to signal that a case statement without a break
is intentional.
remarks:
C compilers have gotten more aggressive about warning when a switch's
 case block does not end with a break or other
flow control statement, flowing into the next case's code, as this is a
common accident that leads to strange bugs. But sometimes falling
through to the next case is the correct and desired behavior. This
symbol lets an app communicate this intention to the compiler, so it
doesn't generate a warning.
It is used like this:
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FILE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro that reports the current file being compiled.
remarks:
This macro is only defined if it isn't already defined, so to
override it (perhaps with something that doesn't provide path
information at all, so build machine information doesn't leak into
public binaries), apps can define this macro before including SDL.h or
 SDL_assert .h.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FLOATWORDORDER
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description:
A macro that reports the target system's floating point word
order.
remarks:
This is set to either SDL_LIL_ENDIAN or SDL_BIG_ENDIAN (and maybe other values in
the future, if something else becomes popular). This can be tested with
the preprocessor, so decisions can be made at compile time.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FLT_EPSILON
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Epsilon constant, used for comparing floating-point numbers.
remarks:
Equals by default to platform-defined FLT_EPSILON , or
 1.1920928955078125e-07F if that's not available.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FORCE_INLINE
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: A macro to demand a function be inlined.
remarks:
This is a command to the compiler to inline a function. SDL uses this
macro in its public headers for a handful of simple functions. On
compilers without forceinline support, this is defined to
 static SDL_INLINE , which is often good enough.

This symbol is used in SDL's headers, but apps and other libraries
are welcome to use it for their own interfaces as well.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FOURCC
macros.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
macros.description: Define a four character code as a Uint32 .
macros.syntax:
#define SDL_FOURCC(A, B, C, D) \
 ((SDL_static_cast(Uint32, SDL_static_cast(Uint8, (A))) << 0) | \
 (SDL_static_cast(Uint32, SDL_static_cast(Uint8, (B))) << 8) | \
 (SDL_static_cast(Uint32, SDL_static_cast(Uint8, (C))) << 16) | \
 (SDL_static_cast(Uint32, SDL_static_cast(Uint8, (D))) << 24))
macros.returns: Returns the four characters converted into a Uint32 , one character per-byte.
macros.threadSafety: It is safe to call this macro from any thread.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_FUNCTION

macros.description: A constant that contains the current function being compiled.

macros.name: SDL_HAS_BUILTIN

macros.description: Check if the compiler supports a given builtin functionality.
remarks:
This allows preprocessor checks for things that otherwise might fail
to compile.

Supported by virtually all clang versions and more-recent GCCs. Use
this instead of checking the clang version if possible.

On compilers without has_builtin support, this is defined to 0
(always false).
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

macros.name: SDL_HAS_TARGET_ATTRIBS

macros.description:
A macro to decide if the compiler supports
 __attribute__((target)) .
remarks:
Even though this is defined in SDL's public headers, it is generally
not used directly by apps. Apps should probably just use SDL_TARGETING directly, instead.
macros.version: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.
